messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following B2B software vendor data processing addendum for GDPR compliance:

"This Data Processing Addendum (DPA) governs processing of Customer Personal Data by Vendor. Vendor processes data solely on Customer's written instructions. Processing activities, data types, and data subject categories are set out in Annex A. Vendor shall implement technical and organisational measures as described in Annex B. Vendor shall not engage sub-processors without prior written approval from Customer. Vendor shall notify Customer of a personal data breach within 24 hours of becoming aware. Vendor shall assist Customer in responding to data subject requests. All processing is performed within the EEA. This DPA shall survive termination of the main agreement for as long as Vendor processes Customer Personal Data."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Correctly structured. The DPA places lawful basis determination with the Customer as Controller. Vendor processes only on written instructions, consistent with Art. 28.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Not explicitly addressed. Special category data handling requirements should be explicitly called out if applicable per Annex A.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Processing limited to Customer instructions with data types and purposes defined in Annex A.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Scope defined in Annex A; Vendor processes only what is instructed.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Survival clause addresses ongoing processing post-termination, but no explicit deletion or return of data obligation upon termination is stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Vendor commits to assist Customer in responding to data subject requests, satisfying Art. 28(3)(e).

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Sub-processors require prior written approval from Customer per Art. 28(2).

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] All processing within EEA stated. No transfer concerns.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PARTIAL] Not addressed in this DPA. Vendor's DPO status and contact should be disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 24-hour notification to Customer exceeds the Art. 33 72-hour supervisory authority requirement.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (special category data not explicitly addressed), Art. 5 (no data deletion obligation on termination)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Add explicit provisions for special category data if Annex A includes such data types
- Add a data deletion and return clause upon contract termination with a defined timeframe
- Disclose Vendor's DPO contact details
